title: Crate scaffold and terminal setup
purpose: Establish the norn-tui crate, terminal capability detection, raw mode management, and DECSTBM scroll region architecture. This is the foundation that every other TUI brief builds on — no rendering, input, or streaming can happen without the terminal being correctly configured and the scroll region established.
task: Create the norn-tui crate at crates/norn-tui/ with Cargo.toml declaring termina, pulldown-cmark, syntect, unicode-width, and tokio dependencies. Create lib.rs with the seven public module declarations (app, input, render, tools, agents, events, terminal). Add norn-tui as a path dependency in norn-cli's Cargo.toml. Modify norn-cli's mode detection to dispatch to norn-tui's entry point when stdin+stdout are TTYs and --print is not set. Implement TerminalCaps detection via termina's capability probing. Implement raw mode enter/exit with a panic hook that restores terminal state. Set up DECSTBM scroll regions on startup and provide a reissue helper for dynamic panel height changes. Implement the fallback path: when DECSTBM or 256-colour are not available, print an explanatory message and delegate to --print mode.

requirements.title: Crate scaffold
requirements.spec: The norn-tui crate SHALL exist as a workspace member with the correct dependency declarations and module structure.
acceptance: crates/norn-tui/ directory exists with Cargo.toml and src/
Cargo.toml declares dependencies: termina, pulldown-cmark, syntect, unicode-width, tokio, norn (path)
Workspace Cargo.toml lists norn-tui as a member
Test: cargo check -p norn-tui succeeds

requirements.title: Module structure
requirements.spec: lib.rs SHALL declare seven public modules matching the DESIGN.md file structure: app, input, render, tools, agents, events, terminal.
acceptance: lib.rs contains pub mod declarations for: app, input, render, tools, agents, events, terminal
Each module directory exists with a mod.rs containing only pub mod declarations
lib.rs exposes a public run_tui() entry point function (signature only — body in NT-011)

requirements.title: norn-cli dependency and mode detection
requirements.spec: norn-cli SHALL depend on norn-tui as a path dependency AND its mode detection SHALL dispatch to norn-tui when stdin and stdout are both TTYs and --print is not set.
acceptance: norn-cli/Cargo.toml lists norn-tui as a path dependency
detect_mode() returns a TUI variant (replacing Repl) when stdin+stdout are TTYs and --print is not set
main.rs dispatches to norn_tui::run_tui() for the TUI mode variant
--print flag still forces print mode regardless of TTY state
Piped stdin or stdout still triggers print mode
Test: detect_mode(print=false, stdin_tty=true, stdout_tty=true) returns TUI mode
Test: detect_mode(print=true, stdin_tty=true, stdout_tty=true) returns Print mode

requirements.title: TerminalCaps detection
requirements.spec: WHEN the TUI starts, THE SYSTEM SHALL probe the terminal for capabilities and store results in a TerminalCaps struct with fields for true_colour, kitty_keyboard, synchronized_rendering, osc_hyperlinks, and italic_support.
acceptance: TerminalCaps struct with bool fields: true_colour, kitty_keyboard, synchronized_rendering, osc_hyperlinks, italic_support
Detection uses termina's capability probing (DA1 response, COLORTERM env var, terminal identification)
true_colour detected via COLORTERM=truecolor/24bit or termina DA1
kitty_keyboard detected via termina's Kitty keyboard protocol probe
synchronized_rendering detected via DCS 2026 support probe
TerminalCaps::detect() returns the populated struct
Test: TerminalCaps fields default to false when constructed without detection

requirements.title: Raw mode and cleanup
requirements.spec: WHEN the TUI starts, THE SYSTEM SHALL enter raw mode AND restore terminal state on exit, including on panic.
acceptance: Terminal enters raw mode via termina on TUI startup
Terminal state is restored on normal exit (drop guard or explicit cleanup)
A custom panic hook restores terminal state before the default panic handler runs
Cleanup restores: cooked mode, cursor visibility, scroll region reset (DECSTBM full screen), line wrap re-enabled
Test: panic cleanup function is callable and does not itself panic

requirements.title: DECSTBM scroll region setup
requirements.spec: WHEN the TUI starts, THE SYSTEM SHALL set a DECSTBM scroll region covering rows 0 through (terminal_height - fixed_panel_height - 1), establishing a scroll region at the top and a fixed panel at the bottom.
acceptance: DECSTBM escape sequence emitted on startup: ESC [ top ; bottom r
Scroll region covers rows 0 through (height - initial_panel_height - 1)
Initial fixed panel height is the minimum: 1 row input + 1 row status bar = 2 rows
Cursor positioned within the scroll region after setup
Test: scroll region setup function produces correct DECSTBM sequence for a given terminal height and panel height

requirements.title: DECSTBM reissue on panel height change
requirements.spec: WHEN the fixed panel height changes (input area grows, agent status lines appear, autocomplete popup opens), THE SYSTEM SHALL reissue DECSTBM with the new boundary AND reposition the cursor appropriately.
acceptance: reissue_scroll_region(new_panel_height) emits the updated DECSTBM sequence
Cursor is saved before reissue and restored after
Content already in the scroll region is preserved (scroll region shrinks from the bottom, not the top)
Test: reissue with a larger panel height produces a smaller scroll region

requirements.title: Capability fallback to print mode
requirements.spec: IF DECSTBM support or 256-colour support cannot be confirmed, THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL print an explanatory message to stderr and fall back to --print mode instead of launching the TUI.
acceptance: When DECSTBM is not supported, a clear message names the missing capability and suggests --print
When 256-colour is not available ($TERM lacks 256color and COLORTERM is unset), same fallback
Fallback delegates to the existing print::run() path in norn-cli
No partial TUI state is left behind (raw mode not entered if fallback fires)
Test: fallback path returns the correct exit code to norn-cli

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement any rendering logic (scroll region writes, fixed panel drawing) — that is NT-002
SHALL NOT implement the event loop or ProviderEvent handling — that is NT-011
SHALL NOT implement input handling — that is NT-004
SHALL NOT remove the existing reedline REPL code — it continues to exist alongside the TUI until the TUI is complete
SHALL NOT add todo!() or unimplemented!() macros anywhere
verification: norn-tui crate compiles as a workspace member
Mode detection returns TUI variant for TTY stdin+stdout with no --print flag
TerminalCaps::detect() returns a valid struct (tested via unit test)
DECSTBM sequence generation verified by unit tests
Panic hook cleanup function does not panic when called
